title: Tragacantha paralipomena
taxonomy: Tragacantha paralipomena is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This classification is due to its characteristics, such as its bean-like fruits and its ability to fix nitrogen in the soil. Further classification places Tragacantha paralipomena within the subfamily Faboideae, which contains a vast array of flowering plants. The genus Tragacantha is thought to be closely related to other genera within the Fabaceae family. As a result, Tragacantha paralipomena is considered a member of the plant kingdom's flowering plant clade.

intro:
There is no information available on Tragacantha paralipomena. However, Tragacanth (Astragalus gummifer) is a plant species with a similar name and properties. 

If we consider Tragacanth (Astragalus gummifer) instead, it is a perennial herb and a natural adhesive derived from the sap of various Astragalus species, commonly known as goat's thorn or gum tragacanth.

The health benefits of Tragacanth include its potential to act as a soothing agent for digestive issues, a gentle laxative, and a demulcent that helps protect and soothe mucous membranes. It is also used to treat conditions such as diarrhea, irritable bowel syndrome, and inflammatory bowel disease.

The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include gum tragacanth, a complex mixture of polysaccharides, and other compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and amino acids. The gum is also known to have emulsifying, thickening, and stabilizing properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include capsules, tablets, and powders that are used to treat digestive issues. It is also used as an ingredient in various food products, such as ice cream, sauces, and beverages, due to its thickening and stabilizing properties.
